active_ux_phase.name: Studio refinement and connectivity-first workflow
expected_outcomes: studio no longer exposes technical graph internals
first Studio surface centered on routes to be served, not strategic helper entities

phase_sequence.name: Decision view and technical tie support

current_focus.stop_reason: governance_stop_max_waves_reached; ux_phase_2 accepted; ux_phase_3 only conceptually opened and must resume in a new authorized cycle

directives: Do not reopen architecture or replace the current Dash/Cytoscape stack.
Use decision_platform as the product surface and preserve Julia-only official execution.
Treat Studio, Runs, Decision and Audit as the main product spaces.
Keep the Studio focused on the business graph only; technical internal hubs, centrais and derived nodes must stay hidden from the primary surface.
Prefer direct manipulation on the canvas over fragmented raw forms whenever the Studio changes.
Treat the initial Studio workflow as route definition first: the operator should draw the routes that must be served and edit route particularities before touching advanced logic.
Do not surface strategic helper entities as editable first-class objects on the initial Studio screen; the first surface should stay centered on business routes and endpoint relationships.
Support route intent in product language, including mandatory, optional and desirable routes, without forcing every route to be a hard constraint.
Prioritize the Studio interaction model before more shell polish: local canvas actions, context menu, reposition/edit flows, and visible supply-chain relationships.
Make who supplies whom explicit on the primary Studio surface; do not force the user to infer business flow from technical metadata.
Do not accept another UX wave that mainly rearranges cards, headers, or wording while routine Studio editing still depends on advanced workbench paths.
Reduce html.Pre and raw JSON as primary UI surfaces; use progressive disclosure instead.
Do not allow orphan nodes or technical clutter in the main final visualization.
Prioritize user-facing flow clarity over backend-only refinements.
Require meaningful UX progress in every wave and stop after three low-value waves.
Push the product toward market-grade UX instead of a cleaned-up engineering console.
acceptance_targets: direct canvas editing for common tasks
evidence_requirements: tests updated where UI behavior changes
visual evidence via screenshot or structured UI snapshot when possible
recent_commit_subjects: wave10 close studio ux phase exit
wave8 add direct studio readiness actions
wave5 add direct studio focus switching
wave4 stabilize selected edge studio flow
